command: if ping -c 3 -W 2 192.168.1.2; then echo "LAN_REACHABLE:true"; else echo "LAN_REACHABLE:false"; exit 1; fi
plaintext_command: if ping -c 3 -W 2 192.168.1.2; then echo "LAN_REACHABLE:true"; else echo "LAN_REACHABLE:false"; exit 1; fi

ability_metadata.ability_description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

output.stdout: PING 192.168.1.2 (192.168.1.2) 56(84) bytes of data.64 bytes from 192.168.1.2: icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=34.2 ms64 bytes from 192.168.1.2: icmp_seq=2 ttl=64 time=20.8 ms64 bytes from 192.168.1.2: icmp_seq=3 ttl=64 time=15.8 ms--- 192.168.1.2 ping statistics ---3 packets transmitted, 3 received, 0% packet loss, time 2002msrtt min/avg/max/mdev = 15.827/23.616/34.193/7.752 msLAN_REACHABLE:true

command: output=$(hping3 --icmp -i u100000 -c 100 7.0.14.254 2>&1); echo "$output"; loss=$(echo "$output" | grep -oP "\d+(?=% packet loss)"); [ -n "$loss" ] && [ "$loss" -lt 95 ] || exit 1
plaintext_command: output=$(hping3 --icmp -i u100000 -c 100 7.0.14.254 2>&1); echo "$output"; loss=$(echo "$output" | grep -oP "\d+(?=% packet loss)"); [ -n "$loss" ] && [ "$loss" -lt 95 ] || exit 1

attack_metadata.technique_name: Network Denial of Service - Direct Network Flood

ability_metadata.ability_description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.
